metadata.seed: Mary's been crying a lot and I'm really tired, so I've needed extra help, you know, with her because I'm so tired and I just can't get out of bed.

patient.query: Yeah, it’s just…some days I feel like I'm carrying this heavy weight. It’s not just about getting out of bed, you know? It’s like, I don’t really see the point sometimes, especially when I feel like I’m not good enough, or that I don’t belong. I just end up feeling more exhausted.

patient.query: Yeah, I guess it’s…just the constant pressure I put on myself. It’s like I have this voice in my head that says I need to be better, do more, or impress people. And when I don’t meet those expectations, it feels like I’m just failing, like I can’t catch a break. That’s when I get really worn out.

When I don’t meet those expectations, I feel this heavy sense of shame, like I’m just not measuring up. It’s really defeating.

patient.query: I mean, if it were a friend, I’d probably tell them that it’s okay to struggle and that they’re not alone in feeling this way. But when I think about what I’d say, it just feels like I’m being a bit of a hypocrite because I can’t really believe that for myself. It's almost like I’m stuck in this cycle where if I don’t feel worthy, I don’t have the motivation to change things, and then that just reinforces the feeling of not being good enough. It’s frustrating.

Instead, it just highlights how far away I feel from that kind of support. It's like I can't give myself the same compassion I’d give to someone else.

patient.query: Yeah, I mean, I guess I’d tell them that it’s okay to not have everything figured out and that everyone has their own struggles, even if it doesn’t show on the surface. Instead, I just feel this nagging sense that I should be doing better, you know? It makes it tough to really believe those words apply to me.

patient.query: I don’t know, it’s just this feeling I’ve carried for so long. It’s like I was always taught that success and achievement were what mattered, so when I look at where I am compared to others, I just feel lacking. Alternative ways to interpret it? I honestly have a hard time seeing those. It feels more real to focus on the gaps rather than any small victories. It’s just easier to stick with what I know, even if it feels heavy.
